title: Border Character Mnemonics
text: Border Character Mnemonics The border character mnemonics define special characters used by Uniface in character mode to render frame borders for entities, area frames, lines, and so on. If these mnemonics are not defined, Uniface uses default characters, generally hyphens (-), vertical bars (|), and plus signs (+). Arguments Table 1. Mnemonics for Border Characters Mnemonic Explanation bot_con Border bottom connector. bot_left Border bottom left corner. bot_line Border bottom line. bot_right Border bottom right corner. mid_con Border middle connector. mid_left Border middle left corner. mid_line Border middle line. mid_right Border middle right corner. top_con Border top connector. top_left Border top left corner. top_line Border top line. top_right Border top right corner. vert_left Border left vertical. vert_mid Border middle vertical. vert_right Border right vertical. Description These characters are usually available in a device character set. If they are defined as part of a particular character set, Uniface sends the control codes to shift into this character set (if it is not already in this font), before displaying or printing the character. For example, these mnemonics are defined as part of character set 2 for the default display. The definition for this set in the USYSTERM device translation table is shown below: BEGIN_SET 2 begin special graphic set ^esc(0 select special graphic set selection ^^ reset reset characterset ... l top_left map top left corner w top_con map top connector k top_right map top right corner q top_line map top line t mid_left map left middle corner n mid_con map middle connector u mid_right map right middle corner q mid_line map middle line m bot_left map bottom left corner v bot_con map bottom middle connector j bot_right map bottom right corner q bot_line map bottom line x vert_left map left vertical line x vert_mid map middle vertical line x vert_rightmap right vertical line ... END_SET Copy To produce a bottom left corner character (identified by the bot_left mnemonic), Uniface first sends the codes associated with the select mnemonic for character set 2, if this character set is not already selected. It then sends the character code associated with the bot_left mnemonic. For example, for the USYSTERM device, the following codes would be sent to the device: ^027(0m Copy Was this topic helpful?
